LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT RELY ON INSURANCE CODE 790.03 TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER B & P CODE 17200. AICCO, INC. V INSURANCE CO. OF NORTH AMERICA (2001) 90 CAL. APP. 4TH 579, 596-597. THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION MAKES CLEAR THAT THE POLICY DID NOT PROVIDE WRONGFUL EVICTION COVERAGE AND SO THERE IS NO COVENANT TO BREACH. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR REFORMATION. A NEGLIGENCE CLAIM CANNOT BE BASED ON THE FACTS ALLEGED. BROWN V. GUARANTEE INS. CO. (1957) 155 CAL. APP. 2D 679, 687-688. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI-MINUTES FOR FEB-04-2008 1:30 PM FOR DEPT 212

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO SET FORTH THE CONDUCT GIVING RISE TO THE SECTION 17200 CLAIM. AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD THE EXISTENCE OF AN INSURANCE CONTRACT OR POLICY AND SET FORTH FACTS AS TO HOW DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT IS A BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION REFORMATION REQUIRES THAT BOTH PARTIES HAVE THE SAME UNDERSTANDING. THAT HAS NOT BEEN ALLEGED. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff may not rely on Insurance Code 790.03 to plead a cause of action under B & P Code 17200. AICCO, Inc. v Insurance Co. of North America (2001) 90 Cal. App. 4th 579, 596-597. The 6th cause of action makes clear that the policy did not provide wrongful eviction coverage and so there is no covenant to breach. Plaintiff does not oppose the demurrer to the 7th cause of action for reformation. A negligence claim cannot be based on the facts alleged. Brown v. Guarantee Ins. Co. (1957) 155 Cal. App. 2d 679, 687-688. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/VC)
